task: openclaw-benben shadow /lite legacy local_direct bypass repair

problem.symptom: Real Feishu /lite returned the old built-in local_direct text mentioning ollama/qwen2.5vl:7b, then ordinary creative writing collapsed to '不知道。'.
problem.root_cause: The global OpenClaw runtime handled bare /lite and /local before the openclaw-benben-vnext plugin could claim the local lane.
problem.post_first_repair_symptom: After bare-command bypass, a real Feishu /lite still returned the old profile-facts card because the existing owner DM session carried persisted routingModeOverride=local_direct and routingLane=local.
problem.post_first_repair_root_cause: The first repair bypassed legacy bare commands but did not ignore legacy local_direct session state already stored in sessions.json.
problem.post_second_repair_symptom: After session-state bypass, a real Feishu /lite was still consumed by the old model shortcut path and ordinary messages ran qwen9b inside the full cloud agent shell, including Memory V4/tool use and timeout recovery.
problem.post_second_repair_root_cause: The vNext before_dispatch hook was not early enough for the gateway agent RPC path; /lite had to be intercepted inside the gateway agent handler before full-agent dispatch.

changes.summary: Created a shadow-owned runtime copy from /usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw so the old production rollback anchor does not run patched code.
Added a shadow-only guard that bypasses legacy bare /lite and /local local_direct interception when OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MARKER=openclaw-benben-shadow or OPENCLAW_SERVICE_KIND=benben-vnext-shadow.
Added the same shadow-only guard for browser/control agent requests.
Removed unused qwen2.5vl local-lane constant from the vNext plugin source.
Added a static regression test for the shadow-only legacy /lite and /local bypass.
In shadow mode, ignore persisted legacy local_direct session state and local lane session suffixes so existing sessions cannot fall back into the old local_direct prompt.
In shadow mode, normalize an existing local_direct routingModeOverride to empty for browser/control agent requests.
Disabled the legacy /lite model-shortcut parser in benben-vNext shadow mode.
Applied the same shadow-only /lite model-shortcut bypass to the directive parser used by reply runtime modules.
Passed session model/routing context into vNext hooks so polluted local model overrides can be detected instead of falling into full-agent cloud memory/tool paths.
Intercepted /lite, /local, and /gemma in the gateway agent handler before full-agent dispatch; subsequent ordinary messages in the marked local lane call local Ollama directly with no cloud fallback.
Moved a bad shadow memory file created by the faulty full-agent /lite continuation to rollback quarantine so future shadow turns do not read it.

rollback.strategy: Restore /etc/systemd/system/openclaw-benben.service and any backed-up drop-ins from backup_dir, run systemctl daemon-reload, then restart openclaw-benben.service. The global /usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw package was not modified.

startup journal shows openclaw-benben-vnext registering Feishu local-lane hooks with serviceKind=benben-vnext-shadow
global /usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist files were not modified by this repair

verification.pending_real_ingress_check: Ask the user to send a fresh Feishu /lite or /lite status message after the 17:38 CST gateway-handler hotfix. Expected reply must be the vNext local-lane text and must not mention qwen2.5vl, Memory V4, or model-switch English.

residual_risk: The old local_direct implementation still exists inside the shadow runtime copy for non-shadow code paths, but bare /lite, /local, and persisted legacy local_direct session state are bypassed for openclaw-benben shadow.
A real Feishu /lite after the 17:38 CST gateway-handler hotfix has not yet been observed; the user sent /new and cloud-lane messages instead.
